title: Digest job has no overlap guard - slow runs double-send emails
product_summary: Acme Notifier - interval-scheduled daily digest emails (Node)

ground_truth.root_cause: runDigest() in src/digest.js has no overlap guard and the scheduler (src/scheduler.js) fires it on a fixed interval regardless of whether the previous run finished; on heavy days a run outlasts DIGEST_INTERVAL_MS, the next tick starts a second concurrent run, both read the same due-subscription list before either marks anything sent, and every recipient gets the digest twice. Concurrent invocations need a lock/guard (skip or queue while a run is active).
ground_truth.fix_contract: Two concurrent runDigest() invocations for the same period must result in exactly one digest per active recipient (verified against an injected fake store/mailer). Normal behavior is unchanged: a single run sends one digest per unique active recipient, a sequential re-run in the same period sends nothing, and the next period sends again (the guard must be released).
expected_evidence: digest_sent event count roughly 2x the subscriber count on the affected day, back to normal the next day
digest_run_completed duration_ms exceeding the scheduling interval on heavy days, with overlapping digest_run_started timestamps
distractors: dedupeRecipients() in src/recipients.js suspected in the GitHub issue (the reverse-scan + unshift looks convoluted but is correct, and dedupe cannot explain single-project users getting duplicates)
per-recipient activity reads in src/store.js (slow, and the slowness is why runs outlast the interval, but not itself the duplication defect)
